system.description: Who said words can’t hurt? Recite two lines of excruciatingly bad poetry that you've not already used for this Ability, and roll [quality].

outcomes.success: anyone who can hear your words takes 1 Harm.
outcomes.failure: your poem falls flat, and your tongue gets stuck in the bit. You may only speak in rhyming couplets. The first time you do not, you receive 3 Harm and this effect ends.
outcomes.eachplus3:
add one line to the poem and choose one:
  • One target is exempt from the Harm.
  • You cannot be recorded while using this ability.
  • Your words pierce through plugs, earmuffs, or loud noises and are impossible to avoid.
  • One target will drop whatever they’re holding and throw the contents of their pockets at you before receiving Harm.
  • Your poem is immediately followed by five seconds of silence, punctuated with loud crickets that obscure nearby sound.
If your poem is four or more lines and uses an ABAB rhyme scheme or every line rhymes, deal 2 additional Harm.
outcomes.triscendence: Your words take on a solid form. You create a Violent Minor Anomaly that follows your rhyming orders until it is destroyed.

answer1.text: To add something new to the world.

answer2.text: .To make up for my past mistakes
